Justification: The kettle shall have a capacity of 1.5 liters, balancing user needs for typical households with enhanced energy efficiency and reduced material usage.; This capacity is selected to optimize energy consumption per boil and reduce the overall material footprint compared to larger capacities, while still accommodating the needs of most households.

The kettle shall boil 1.5 liters of water from ambient temperature (20°C) to boiling point (100°C) within approximately 6 minutes.; This target boiling time balances user satisfaction with energy efficiency, allowing for a lower wattage design and reduced overall energy consumption over the product's lifetime.; The boiling time for a minimum fill (0.5 liters) shall not exceed 3 minutes.

The kettle shall have a power rating of 1800W to provide a balance between boiling speed and energy efficiency over the product's lifetime.; This power rating ensures efficient operation without excessive strain on standard household electrical circuits.

The exterior casing shall be constructed from 100% recycled stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade), sourced responsibly to ensure high recycled content and ethical manufacturing.; Bioplastics are excluded due to unproven durability, complex end-of-life scenarios, and potential for premature failure. The recycled stainless steel will feature a durable, easy-to-maintain finish that resists scratching and fading.

The interior shall be made of 100% recycled stainless steel (e.g., 304 grade), ensuring food-grade safety and durability.; The heating element shall be a distinct, easily removable, and replaceable component made from durable, recyclable materials. Its connection to the interior will not create a composite that hinders recycling, and it will be designed for easy disassembly.

PDS Element: Use of Material / Number of Materials
Justification: The total number of distinct material types shall be minimized, prioritizing 100% recycled stainless steel for all primary components.; For any non-contact, non-critical parts (e.g., internal wiring insulation), a durable, well-characterized, and easily recyclable polymer will be used. All materials will be clearly defined, verified for source and recyclability, and meet food-grade and BPA-free requirements.
The kettle shall consume less than 0.5W in standby mode.; Heat retention of the kettle body will be optimized through material selection and design to reduce energy use during operation.

The auto shut-off mechanism shall be robust, reliable over the product's lifespan, and precise to prevent over-boiling and energy waste.; The mechanism will activate when boiling point is reached or when the kettle is lifted from its base.

A robust and reliable boil-dry protection mechanism shall be implemented, tested for longevity (minimum 3,000 cycles) to prevent premature product failure.; This feature automatically shuts off the heating element if no water is detected.

The kettle shall be designed for a minimum lifespan of 3,000 boiling cycles, achieved through robust component selection (thermostat, switch, heating element), high-quality materials, and design considerations for wear and tear.; Critical spare parts, including the heating element, shall be available for at least 7-10 years to facilitate repair and extend product life.

The kettle shall withstand a 0.5-meter drop onto a hard surface without catastrophic failure (e.g., shattering, loss of structural integrity, or function).; The handle, base, and lid hinge will be designed for impact resistance, prioritizing ease of disassembly over extreme impact resistance if it compromises circularity.

The handle shall be ergonomic, heat-resistant, and non-slip, constructed from recycled stainless steel with an integrated durable grip material.; The grip material will be chosen for its compatibility with stainless steel recycling or ease of separation. Separate insulation layers will be avoided.
The spout shall be drip-free and designed for easy cleaning to prevent limescale buildup and maintain hygiene.; The connection to the interior shall be smooth and accessible.

The lid opening mechanism and hinge must be robust and durable for thousands of operations, designed to prevent premature failure.; Materials and design considerations will focus on high-wear components to ensure longevity.

The interior shall be designed for easy manual cleaning and descaling, with accessible surfaces and heating element.; Materials shall resist limescale buildup and staining. Components will not be designated as dishwasher-safe if it compromises durability or disassembly.

The water level indicator shall be made from materials that resist fogging and discoloration over time, remaining visible and accurate throughout the product's life.; It will be protected from direct steam and designed for easy manual cleaning.

Cord storage shall be designed to be durable and not put undue stress on the power cord or its connection point to the base, which are common failure points.; The mechanism will allow for neat storage and easy access.

The target noise level shall be 60 dB during boiling, with noise reduction methods not compromising durability, repairability, or the use of sustainable materials.; Noise consistency throughout the boiling cycle will be confirmed.

The kettle shall feature durable, easy-to-maintain finishes and materials that resist scratching, fading, and water spots to ensure the product maintains its appearance over its lifespan.; Design will prioritize a clean, minimalist appearance with functional integration of components.

Packaging shall utilize 100% recycled and recyclable materials, minimizing volume and weight.; Plastic tapes or coatings that hinder recyclability will be avoided, opting for water-based adhesives where possible. Packaging will provide adequate protection without being excessive.

The kettle shall be designed for disassembly using mechanical fasteners (screws, clips) wherever possible, avoiding adhesives and composite materials that cannot be easily separated.; All materials will be clearly labeled with standard recycling codes. The heating element will be a distinct, easily removable component. Disassembly will not require specialized tools.

The kettle shall meet relevant safety and energy efficiency certifications, including Energy Star (aiming for the highest efficiency tiers).; Certifications related to material sustainability and ethical manufacturing for the entire product lifecycle will be pursued.

A durable indicator light (e.g., LED) with a long lifespan shall be specified. Its failure must not compromise product function or safety.; The design will ensure easy replacement if possible, and its operation will be subtle enough not to be distracting.

The base shall be stable and non-slip with durable electrical contacts, designed for disassembly to allow separation of electronic components from structural materials for recycling.; The connection between the kettle and base will be robust and designed for easy disconnection at end-of-life.
Standard screws, common electrical connectors, and off-the-shelf components (thermostats, heating elements) will be used where possible to reduce tooling costs, lead times, and inventory complexity.

Parts will be designed for easy assembly with clear orientation, appropriate snap-fits, and chamfered edges, avoiding designs requiring complex jigs or excessive force. Simple integration and sealing of components like the water level indicator and cord storage will be ensured.

High-volume, repeatable, and cost-effective fabrication processes such as deep drawing, stamping, and injection molding will be selected. Processes will minimize post-processing and be compatible with recycled materials.
